edge_hypothesis: ema_rsi_atr has 2 ACCEPTs (PF=1.327 and PF=1.270), both at 10:1 R:R with narrow RSI bands near 50. The R:R CEILING for this template is UNKNOWN — nobody has tested beyond 10:1. The underlying mechanism is COMPRESSION ENTRY: when RSI is near 50 during an EMA uptrend, price is consolidated, volatility is contracted, and the ATR gate confirms above-average volatility is beginning (the breakout from compression). These entries SHOULD produce outsized tails because compressed price action resolves into multi-ATR directional moves. At 12:1 R:R, the TP is 18.0x ATR — this requires catching the full extent of a compression breakout that travels 18+ ATR units. On 1h ETH, that's a 3-5% move. On 4h ETH, that's a 5-10% move. Crypto regularly produces these moves after consolidation. THREE HYPOTHESES: (1) R:R SCALES: If PF > 1.2 at 12:1 (vs PF=1.327 at 10:1), compression entries have deep tails and ema_rsi_atr's R:R peak is above 10:1. This would make ema_rsi_atr the system's HIGHEST VIABLE R:R template. (2) R:R PEAKED: If PF drops to 1.0-1.1 at 12:1, the 10:1 peak is confirmed — tails beyond 15x ATR are too rare for this signal. The template is optimized and should be deployed at 10:1. (3) R:R COLLAPSED: If PF < 0.9 at 12:1, compression breakouts have a hard tail limit around 15x ATR — the market rarely sustains moves beyond this from a compressed state. Stop testing higher R:R for ema_rsi_atr. Also testing a 1.25x TIGHT STOP variant — if compression entries have high directional conviction, tighter stops should HELP (faster cut on wrong entries, more capital for winners). Invalidation: If PF < 0.8 at 12:1 with tight stops across all variants, 10:1 is the confirmed ceiling.

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_ceiling_1h_12to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on ETH/1h with 12:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 18.0x ATR TP). The flagship R:R ceiling test. The existing ACCEPT at 10:1 had PF=1.327 with 162 trades. At 12:1, TP moves from 15.0x to 18.0x ATR — an additional 3x ATR distance. The question: what fraction of 10:1 winners ALSO travel an extra 3x ATR? If the compression-breakout mechanism produces fat-tailed returns (which it should, since compression resolves into trending), many 10:1 winners should ALSO be 12:1 winners. Breakeven: 7.7% win rate. Expected: 162 trades (same entry signal), win rate 8-12%. PF target > 1.2.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope is positive (current EMA_50 > previous bar EMA_50).
ATR_14 > rolling 50-bar ATR mean (volatility expanding — breakout beginning).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope is negative (current EMA_50 < previous bar EMA_50).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
EMA trend: Close > EMA_200, EMA_50 slope positive
RSI compression: RSI_14 <= 50 (near midline = consolidated momentum)
ATR breakout: ATR_14 > 50-bar rolling mean (volatility expansion starting)
R:R ceiling test: 12:1 — does the compression tail extend beyond 10:1?
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 12:1. Needs 7.7% win rate to break even after fees.
Ceiling test: do compression breakouts produce 18x ATR tails?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — standard ema_rsi_atr baseline
RSI_14 <= 50 compression entry — near-midline = consolidated price action
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 18.0x TP — 12:1 R:R ceiling test, STOP_FLOOR compliant

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_ceiling_4h_12to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on ETH/4h with 12:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop / 18.0x ATR TP). The 4h variant of the R:R ceiling test. On 4h, the ATR represents larger absolute price moves — 18x ATR on 4h means targeting 5-10% directional moves from compression. ETH regularly produces 5-10% moves over 2-5 day periods following consolidation. The 4h timeframe should FAVOR higher R:R because larger timeframe = larger moves = deeper tails. If 4h/12:1 produces higher PF than 1h/12:1, it confirms that timeframe amplifies tail depth for compression entries. Expected: 50-80 trades (4h reduces frequency), PF target > 1.3.

entry_short: EMA_50 slope is negative (current EMA_50 < previous bar EMA_50).

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_tightstop_1h_10to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on ETH/1h with TIGHT stop: 1.25x ATR stop / 12.5x ATR TP (10:1 R:R). Same R:R as the existing ACCEPT (10:1) but with a tighter stop (1.25x vs 1.5x). The CONVICTION thesis: compression entries have high directional conviction at the moment of entry — if RSI is near 50 and ATR is expanding and the EMA trend is established, the breakout direction should be correct more often than random. A tighter stop at 1.25x ATR captures this conviction: wrong entries are cut 17% faster (1.25 vs 1.5), preserving capital. Right entries proceed unchanged since they move AWAY from the stop immediately. If PF > 1.4 (vs existing 1.327 at 1.5x/10:1), tighter stops improve ema_rsi_atr. If PF < 1.2, the 1.5x standard stop is needed — some compression entries initially dip before resolving.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.

Tight stop test: 1.25x ATR (vs standard 1.5x) — conviction-based stop width
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.25x ATR).
R:R = 10:1. Needs 9.1% win rate to break even after fees.
Conviction test: do compression entries have enough directional accuracy for tighter stops?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.25x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.5x ATR_14 from entry.
